title: WiseTech Global Limited — Regulatory action
investigation_objective: The ACCC accepted a court-enforceable undertaking (accepted 30 December 2025, announced 5 January 2026) requiring WiseTech Global Limited and its subsidiary BluJay Solutions (Australia) Pty Ltd to divest Expedient, a logistics software business acquired as part of WiseTech's e2open acquisition completed in August 2025, to an ACCC-approved buyer capable of operating it as a viable competitor to WiseTech's CargoWise platform. The ACCC found WiseTech completed the acquisition before its competition review had concluded despite being aware the deal raised significant competition concerns - a 'gun-jumping' issue under Australia's new mandatory pre-completion merger notification regime, which took effect 1 January 2026.
scope: Subject to a court-enforceable undertaking requiring divestiture of an acquired business after an ACCC competition investigation; Regulator found the underlying acquisition was completed before its competition review concluded, despite awareness of competition concerns; Matter arises under Australia's new mandatory pre-completion merger notification regime
